basics.description: Deploying the full Keysight CloudLens stack in a single click. This stands up vController (control plane), KVO (orchestrator), and vPB (data plane) into a shared VNet with five purpose-built subnets. Total deploy time is about 15 minutes. After deploy, the outputs include a one-line Cloud Shell command that installs CloudLens sensors on tagged workload VMs.

location.label: Region
location.toolTip: Azure region for all three VMs and the shared VNet. Pick the region closest to your monitored workloads.

options.text: **About the prefix:** the name you pick becomes the prefix for every VM and resource. The VMs will be `<prefix>-vcontroller`, `<prefix>-kvo`, `<prefix>-vpb`. For multi-region deployments, use a region-suffixed prefix like `cloudlens-eastus` so each region's stack has unique names.

elements.name: namePrefix
elements.label: Name prefix
elements.toolTip: Used as the prefix for all VMs, NICs, NSGs, public IPs, and the shared VNet. 3 to 32 characters, lowercase letters, digits, hyphens; must start and end with a letter or digit.

constraints.validationMessage: 3 to 32 characters. Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens. Must start and end with a letter or digit.

elements.name: adminUsername
elements.label: Linux admin username
elements.toolTip: OS-level admin user for SSH. Shared across vController, KVO, and vPB.

options.text: Heads-up: the password below is for **OS-level SSH** on all three VMs. The vController **web UI** uses built-in credentials (`admin / Cl0udLens@dm!n`) you'll change on first login. The KVO **web UI** admin account is set during first-login EULA acceptance. The vPB **CLI** is reached through a two-hop SSH and has its own credentials. All separate from this password.

toolTip.password: Shared OS-level password for all three VMs. 12+ chars, upper, lower, digit, symbol. NOT the web UI password.

elements.name: vcontrollerVmSize
elements.label: vController VM size
elements.toolTip: vController size. Standard_D4s_v5 is sized for most deployments. Scale up for thousands of sensors.

allowedValues.label: Standard_D16s_v5 (16 vCPU, 64 GB, very large fleets)

elements.name: kvoVmSize
elements.label: KVO VM size
elements.toolTip: KVO size. Standard_D4s_v5 is sized for most deployments.

elements.name: vpbVmSize
elements.label: vPB VM size
elements.defaultValue: Standard_D8s_v3 (8 vCPU, 32 GB, minimum for 3 NICs)
elements.toolTip: vPB size. Minimum Standard_D8s_v3 is required for three accelerated NICs.

allowedValues.label: Standard_D8s_v3 (8 vCPU, 32 GB, minimum for 3 NICs)
Standard_D16s_v3 (16 vCPU, 64 GB, higher mirror throughput)

options.text: The stack creates ONE shared VNet with five subnets: vcontroller-subnet (10.50.1.0/24), kvo-subnet (10.50.2.0/24), vpb-mgmt (10.50.10.0/24), vpb-ingress (10.50.11.0/24), vpb-egress (10.50.12.0/24). Override the address space below if your default range conflicts.

elements.name: vnetAddressSpace
elements.label: Shared VNet address space (CIDR)
elements.toolTip: CIDR for the shared VNet. The five subnets carve from this. Must not overlap with VNets you plan to peer with.
